title: melampyrum barbatum subsp. carstiense
intro: Melampyrum barbatum subsp. carstiense, also known as the hairy fumewort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Orobanche, characterized by its yellow or greenish-yellow flowers and hairy stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, particularly in Eastern Europe, where its roots are believed to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Melampyrum barbatum subsp. carstiense include its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as a natural remedy for digestive problems and fever reduction. In herbal preparations, the roots of the plant are typically dried and then made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
